content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer A (2≤A≤109)A \ (2 \leq A \leq 10^{9})A (2≤A≤109). Find two integers BBB and CCC such that:


2≤A<B<C≤10182 \leq A \lt B \lt C \leq 10^{18}2≤A<B<C≤1018;
ABA^{B}AB %\%% C=0;C = 0;C=0;
BCB^{C}BC %\%% A=0A = 0A=0
It can be proved that at least one solution exists. If multiple solutions exist, print any.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer AAA as mentioned in statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output two space-separated integers BBB and CCC satisfying the conditions.


It can be proved that at least one solution exists. If multiple solutions exist, print any.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10^4), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer A (2 ≤ A ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two space‑separated integers B and C satisfying 2 ≤ A < B < C ≤ 10^18, A^B mod C = 0 and B^C mod A = 0. Print each pair on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Special case A=2. B=4, C=8. 2^4=16 is divisible by 8 and 4^8 is divisible by 2.
Applying the construction for each A. All values respect the limits and satisfy the two modular conditions.

solution.approach:
We need B to be a multiple of A so that B^C is automatically divisible by A. The simplest choice is B = 2·A (always > A and ≤ 2·10^9). For the first condition we need C to divide A^B. Since A^B is a pure power of A, any divisor must be a power of A. Choose C = A^2, which is ≤ 10^18 because A ≤ 10^9. For A ≥ 3 we have A^2 > 2·A, so A < B < C holds. The only problematic value is A = 2, where A^2 = 4 equals B = 4. In that case we use C = A^3 = 8, still within limits. The construction:
if A == 2: B = 4, C = 8
else: B = 2*A, C = A*A
Both conditions are satisfied:
- A^B contains A^2 as a factor, so A^B mod C = 0.
- B is a multiple of A, therefore B^C mod A = 0.
The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
